pi_items.source: Task description directly lists these 5 tables, and verifier.py strictly expects their existence.

verifier.py strictly enforces these exact minimum bounds. Data analysis of the Oracle HR schema source file confirms the actual row counts exceed these minimums (e.g., 107 employees, 11 departments with employees, 19 jobs).
Task description requests saving the analytical report here, and export_result.sh explicitly checks for this file.
Task description requests saving row counts here, and export_result.sh explicitly checks for this file.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the standard synthetic Oracle HR Sample Schema (db-sample-schemas). The agent must create five specific tables: DIM_DEPARTMENT, DIM_JOB, DIM_EMPLOYEE, DIM_TIME, and FACT_WORKFORCE. Analysis of the underlying SQL data and evaluation logic (verifier.py) confirms that the dataset contains enough records to meet all minimum row count thresholds (11 for departments, 19 for jobs, 100 for employees/fact_workforce, and 5 for time). The required output files for the final evaluation are correctly identified as /home/ga/Desktop/warehouse_analysis.txt and /home/ga/Desktop/warehouse_counts.txt.
